5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Everyone is racing to build smarter AI models. Very few organizations are learning how to build better AI systems.
The AI Demo Looked Amazing
The executive team gathered around a conference room screen.
A developer typed a question.
The AI responded instantly.
It summarized documents, answered technical questions, generated code, and even explained complex business processes. The demo ended with applause, excitement, and a familiar sentence:
"Let's deploy this into production."
Three months later, the excitement had disappeared.
The chatbot produced inconsistent answers.
Employees stopped trusting it.
Customers found factual mistakes.
Operational costs were much higher than expected.
Engineers spent more time fixing prompts than building new features.
Eventually, the project quietly became another abandoned experiment.
If you've worked on enterprise AI, this story probably sounds familiar.
